Upper Volume
Recorded Sayings at Shengshou Chan Monastery
Recorded Sayings at Benjue Chan Monastery

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Recorded Sayings of the Master Yungu, Upper Volume

cb:div.cb:mulu: Recorded Sayings of the Master Yungu at His First Residence at Shengshou Chan Monastery in Pingjiang Prefecture
cb:div.head: Recorded Sayings of the Master Yungu at His First Residence at Shengshou Chan Monastery in Pingjiang Prefecture
cb:div.p:
In the eighth month of the fourth year of Baoyou, the master entered the monastery and, pointing to the three gates, said: One who has the power to pull open and fully penetrate
and open the eyes still has not yet entered the gate of Shengshou. Today the new abbot lets it pass. He shouted!

In the Buddha Hall, do not seek the Buddha, do not seek the Dharma—what use is making prostrations? Lifting up the sitting mat, he said: Let go
—it must not be let pass.

Occupying the room like Mojie, closing the room like Vimalakirti. Cutting off words, he struck the bed and said: The thirty-six thousand qing of Lake Tai,
with the moon in the heart of the waves—toward whom is it spoken?

Picking up the robe: At Jizu Peak it is firmly upheld, at Dayu Ridge it cannot be lifted. Raising the Dharma robe, he said: This
—what is it? Do you take me to be concealing anything? I conceal nothing from you.

Ascending the seat, he held up incense and said: This single piece of incense is respectfully for
Praying for the present emperor, may the sacred lifespan be prolonged: ten thousand years, ten thousand years, ten thousand times ten thousand years!
Then he took his seat and gave instruction, saying: Across vast misty waves, at the four bridges with wind and moon, a thousand-foot line is cast straight down
hanging; the great turtle of the three mountains gives a slight tug. Is there any who does not crave the fragrant bait?問答不錄

Before the main thread is raised, its pattern not yet revealed—there is Suzhou, there is Changzhou—just a trace appears. The main mountain is high, the table
mountain is low; nowhere does it remain, everywhere it is accomplished. In the bustling dusty market, letting go two, casting off three,
amid piled white waves, displaying the oar and dancing the rudder. South to Chuihong, west to Hepu, you all come and go,
you all nod your heads. Just as when Baoshou opened the hall and Sansheng pushed out a monk, Baoshou immediately struck him,
what was the intent? Holding the Mo-ye sword crosswise to carry out the proper command, in a peaceful realm cutting down the foolish and obstinate.

At the evening informal instruction, after questions and answers, he said: Deshan in informal instruction would not answer questions, displaying the might that split Mount Tai
with giant strength; Zhaozhou in informal instruction required answers, unfolding the power of a golden-winged bird cleaving the vast sea. If one
can raise one and illuminate three, with eyes and faculties balanced to the finest degree, like a magnet meeting iron, with a light pull it
moves—why speak of giving thirty blows? Why speak of throwing a brick to attract jade? Tonight no merit is set in motion, no
influence is stirred; the tracks are changed and the thoroughfare opened, the sea routes run in order. Do you understand? Lake Tai spans three regions.

Again he cited: Bao Gong had someone convey a message to Sida: Why not come down the mountain to teach and transform living beings? Why gaze
at the Milky Way? Sida said: The Buddhas of the three times have all been swallowed by me in a single gulp—what living beings
are there to be transformed? The master commented: These two elders—one with sweet words but a bitter heart, the other who, once supported, becomes
drunk. In the teaching tradition this may be acceptable, but under the gate of patch-robed monks, it must drip with blood
drop by drop. He struck the staff once and said: The cart is not pushed sideways; principle admits no crooked cutting.

Ascending the hall: For twenty days since entering the monastery, busy and bustling. Meeting monks, I offer deep greetings; seeing laypeople, I bow low
in salutation. Why is it that of ten there are five pairs? Missing the moment of opportunity. Even so, misfortune does not enter the careful household
door.

On the Buddha's nirvana, ascending the hall: When arising and ceasing are extinguished, quiescent extinction is bliss; the yellow-faced Gautama, having died, cannot live
again. When the assembly was told and the coffin displayed the crossed feet, the yellow-faced Gautama, though alive, cannot die. Shengshou
has a secretly transmitted method: that which dies can live, that which lives can die. Then he took up the staff and struck
it once and said: Alive, alive! Do you all still see old Gautama? Leaning on the staff, he said: No
Is it so, or is it not?

Thanking Head Seat Jing of Tiantai and Storehouse Master Wang of Jingci, ascending the hall: In Jiangnan and the Two Zhe regions, spring is cold and autumn
is hot. Clearly speaking it, one does not know it exists; clearly knowing it exists, one cannot speak it. After a pause, he said: Fenggan
riding that great tiger was originally the white-brow of South Mountain.

Ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked Fengxue: Speech and silence both involve subtle deviation—how can one pass through without transgressing? Fengxue said:
I often recall Jiangnan in the third month, where partridges cry and a hundred flowers are fragrant. The master commented: The yellow-faced one of Zhe
chants a single sacred spell, binding all the people of the great earth completely, so that they cannot even breathe
at all. Shengshou cannot avoid reciting the universal transference mantra: Reaching the end of Jiang and Wu lands, across the shore the Yue mountains
are many.

Engraved and entered among precious works. Ascending the hall: The true Dharma eye of Vulture Peak, Zhongfeng broke the earthen basin. Supported, it cannot
be raised; shaken, it stirs heaven and earth. He gave a shout and said: I walk in the wild grasses, while you enter the deep village.

Ascending the hall: The fire of mind blazes, the winds of conditions surge. At Xiande Mountain, Linji displayed great function and great application. Breaking
through the toiling dust of beings, overturning them seven or eight times. Do you understand? A good merchant does not keep dead goods.

Thanking Head Seat Wuwai, ascending the hall: When a single leaf falls, all under heaven is autumn—if you understand in this way, how can it suffice? When a
speck of dust arises, the whole earth is contained—if you do not understand in this way, how can it suffice? Whether so or not so, iron walls upon
iron walls. Even so, if one is not a clear-eyed person, it is very difficult to discern fully.

Thanking Storehouse Master Xiang of Jingshan and Attendant Hang of Wenzhou, ascending the hall: The entire Tripitaka teaching is like cutting a begging bowl
to pieces, filling the mouth with ice and frost; personally arriving at Caoqi, circling the seat and shaking the staff, thorns on level ground. Raising it
has been raised, picking it up has been picked up. Do you understand? Monks of Sichuan are rough, monks of Zhe are at ease.

Ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked Xianglin: What is the matter beneath the patched robe? Xianglin said: In the twelfth month, a fire
burns the mountain. The master commented: In the twelfth month, a fire burns the mountain—words are direct and speech is also direct. White clouds gather at the valley mouth,
the bright moon soaks the railing.

On the eighth day of the twelfth month, heavy snow fell; the master Yanxi of Jingshan arrived. Ascending the hall: Two thousand years ago, two thousand years
later—marvelous, marvelous! Rare, truly rare! All alike go out from the mountain and come down to teach and transform beings—are there
any whose eyes are clear and whose minds are awakened? If not, Shengshou will add frost upon snow. He struck the staff
the staff, then descended from the seat.

On New Year's Day, ascending the hall: Speaking on the first day of the first month today, there are many taboos in speech. If one speaks of the Buddha Dharma, it is said:
If you utter the word Buddha, rinse your mouth for three days. If one speaks of worldly matters, it is said: Under the patriarchs' gate, it is not permitted
to engage in vulgar talk. He lifted the staff and said: Let the staff reveal a bit of news. He struck it once—oxen advance by the thousand,
and horses advance by the hundred.

On the Lantern Festival, ascending the hall: The moon in the sky is full, in the human world the moon is half; one lamp becomes hundreds of thousands of millions of lamps, everywhere
the light shines brilliantly. Suddenly mist gathers and clouds arise, heaven and earth turn pitch black—where is the lamp
then? After a pause, he said: I recall Xianglin—proving a turtle becomes a soft-shelled turtle.

Ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked Yunmen: Luminous still illumination pervades sands like the Ganges. Yunmen said: Is this not
the words of the scholar Zhang Zhuo? The monk said: Yes. Yunmen said: You have fallen into words. The master commented: Before the spark strikes,
within the flash of lightning, if you try to trace it, it is a thousand or ten thousand miles away. Even so, where is it
that this monk has fallen into words? After a pause, he said: On the road nothing is lost and found; the noble person praises this.

Ascending the hall: The spring days linger, the spring colors cling; seeing peach blossoms, there is no more doubt; hearing bamboo struck, one forgets
what was known. Why is it that one person says they have not understood, another says they have not penetrated? After a pause, he said: Fine gold from the great smelter
should show no change in color.

Reading the sayings of Fohai of Jingshan, ascending the hall: The single transmission at Vulture Peak, the direct pointing at Shaolin. Beginning from Shengyuan Pavilion
and ending at Lingxiao Peak, in between these two it has never been spoken. Do you all know
where Fohai lands? The pattern is already revealed.

Thanking the Storehouse Master and the Assistant Supervisor, ascending the hall: The entire Tripitaka teaching—how can it compare to the yellowed pages on a chest? These
words cannot be explained except by a drowsy tiger, cannot be displayed except by a seller of fresh ginger. As for the rest,
those who say black beans are good for making paste and white rice good for cooking—Shengshou will not nod in approval. Why
is this? The home is in the western region.

At the summer retreat, ascending the hall: He lifted the staff and struck it once—if you grasp it here, the assembly at Vulture Peak
remains solemnly unscattered; if not, then listen to the staff speak the verse of the retreat. He struck it once
and said: Too much ornamentation arises.

Ascending the hall: Baizhang encountered Mazu’s shout, Linji received Huangbo’s blows—piercing the skull, glued basin, throwing the body
into the net of law. As for this side and that side, three hundred or five hundred, vast discussions—beneath the skin is there still
blood? He struck the staff once and said: The iron whip has great ძალ, yet laments having no foe.

Ascending the hall: The patched-robed monk’s equipment is the eyes of pilgrimage, revealing the upward nose. Lifting the vajra hammer, it shatters
the nests of sages and ordinary ones. Wielding the sword of wisdom, it cuts off the lifeline of Buddhas and patriarchs. The entire Tripitaka is but
tattered old paper for wiping sores. The thousand seven hundred transmission lamps are merely idle implements for playing monkeys. He gave a shout,
yet a good cause does not necessarily bring a bad result.

Ascending the hall: Bodhidharma faced the wall, Yunmen recited seven. Thunder crashes on dry ground, the blazing sun in scorching heat. The ancients
were thus, but Shengshou is not thus. One blow leaves one welt, one slap leaves a palmful of blood. What is the intent?
If the sixth month is not hot, the five grains will not ripen.

At the lifting of restrictions, ascending the hall: For ten thousand miles not an inch of grass, yet upon leaving the gate there is grass. A swift hawk soars, a fierce tiger
blocks the path. He struck the bed and said: Even clear-eyed monks cannot see through it.

Mid-Autumn rain, ascending the hall: The level lake lies dim, clouds envelop the green peaks. Not knowing the mind-moon is solitary and full, one often
greedily gazes at the sky. Have you all awakened sharply? It is just right for practice, just right for making offerings.

Rebuilding the Yuanzhao Hall and the abbot’s quarters, ascending the hall: One zhang deep, one zhang wide, a model handled by Buddhas and patriarchs through past and present.
As for the Yuanzhao Hall, last night it contended forehead to forehead with the Sangao Hall—what number case is this?
After a pause, he said: It is hard to deceive the many eyes.

Opening the furnace, ascending the hall: Sitting in meditation before the holy monks, preaching to clay images. The old fire from two thousand years ago
has already been deeply stirred open. Do you all feel sweat pouring over your whole bodies? If not, wait until it cools
and then see.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Recorded Sayings at Benjue Chan Monastery in Jiaxing Prefecture
cb:div.head: Recorded Sayings at Benjue Chan Monastery in Jiaxing Prefecture
cb:div.p:
Sitting in the room, he pressed the staff crosswise and said: The full command is upheld like an iron wall. The iron wall—he struck the staff once
and said: Baizhang is deaf, Huangbo sticks out his tongue.

Holding up the document: The treasury of the true Dharma eye, entrusted at Vulture Peak, is right here today. What is the matter of today?
The document is issued from Jiaxing Prefecture.

Ascending the hall: Phrases that cannot be spoken even with full effort have already been spoken three times in the hall, and spoken once with a single strike in the pavilion
as well. Some, hearing it said this way, bite off their thumbs. Some, hearing it said this way, furrow both
brows. Even so, one gains and one loses.

Ascending the hall: Thanking the master Long’an, and also Zhou the compiler, the West Hall of Daming, and Head Seat Xingsheng
attendant—once with a single assent, Kashyapa smiled; a blaze that consumes the plains arises from a single spark; down to carrying
Muzhou’s board and lifting the broken basin, all are like pouring oil to save a fire, making the flames grow ever stronger. He gave a shout,
and said: In broad daylight under the blue sky, is there such a thing?

A posthumous letter from the master Jieshi of Jingci arrived. Ascending the hall: The Dharma of impermanence and birth and death stands abruptly like the southern mountain
leaning. At the edge of the sky it has nothing to do with me; the rippling West Lake soaks the cold moon. Further speaking a parting verse,
eight or nine parts clear, seventy-two; empty space becomes two sides, where there is little it is made full, where there is much it is added to. This
is the old man Jieshi’s mistaken offering in death; today at Shoushan it is completely turned to life. Do you understand? If
you let the tears fall frequently, even the great ocean would have to dry up.

Thanking the Storehouse Master, ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked the master Wuzu: The entire Tripitaka is a cutting foot—
but what character does it cut? Wuzu said: boluoniang. A verse says: In one phrase the Sanskrit text is fully raised,
the tongue manifests clearly with resonant tones. Not knowing, one takes it as level and oblique, but in the end it turns out oblique
oblique level.

Ascending the hall: This phrase is cut away, that which is not a phrase is also cut away, forcing a rooster to lay swan’s eggs. In this way
it is acceptable; not in this way it is also acceptable; put down the dead snake and pick up a live scorpion. Originally speaking thus, the intent lies
where? The founding master Jinghui personally saw old Longya.

Ascending the hall, he cited: The master Ying’an asked the master Mi’an: What is the true Dharma eye? Mi’an
said: A broken earthen basin. The master commented: The old man Mi’an, before the hall of great awakening, turned aside and violated a taboo,
so that the true lineage was swept into the dust. Do you all know? The aeon stone is easy to wear away, but local speech is hard to change.

On the memorial day of Fohai, holding up incense, he said: Hastening in the wind before Taibai Peak, words are spoken to the left; then again attending
At the assembly on Vulture Peak, a great thunder pierced the ears. From then on resentment coiled, also wanting to bring disgrace upon his gate
and household. Old master! I do not value your loosening of bonds, only your adding of fetters; I do not approve your complete gathering in,
only approve your complete release. Earnestly burning this incense, this is called true Dharma offering.

Thanking Lin Tiguan of Lin’an, Attendant Ying of Jingshan, Attendant Jian of Wanshou, and Storehouse Master Jun of Lingyin,
ascending the hall: Vimalakirti’s single silence, the national teacher’s three calls. Clouds gather in the blue sky, thunder crashes through the great void. As for
the entire Tripitaka, is there still this message or not? Bah! Within the city of Chang’an, do not ask about
the imperial capital.

Opening the furnace, ascending the hall: Cold and icy, once the ground thaws—six years in the snowy mountains, nine years at Mount Song, not even half is attained.
This mountain monk intends to carry it out, yet the Dharma is exhausted and there are no people. For now, harmonize the light and mingle with the dust, making the fire
warm and heated. After a pause, he said: How does the breadth of the furnace compare to the breadth of an ancient mirror?

Ascending the hall, he cited: While Yaoshan was sitting, a monk asked: What are you thinking in this still, fixed way? Yaoshan said: Thinking
of that which does not think. The monk said: How do you think of that which does not think? Yaoshan said: Not thinking
is it. A verse says: A gnarled ancient tree leans in the cold forest, snow presses and frost buries, the signs of spring lie deep; suddenly a fine
breeze gently stirs it—listen closely and it is entirely the roar of a dragon.

On inaugurating the sacred festival, ascending the hall: A single phrase without self is revealed in the open. Lightning coils around the stellar axis, a rainbow flows
over the splendid islet. Throughout heaven and earth, sentient and insentient alike all rejoice and dance. Why is this? When the sage
acts, all things behold.

Thanking the master Xisou of Fahua. Head Seat Yuanbie, ascending the hall, cited: Jueyin of Langya went to visit Fahua
and was asked: Where have you recently come from? He said: The Two Zhe regions. Did you come by boat or by land? He said: By boat.
Where is the boat? He said: How do you speak a phrase that does not tread the path step by step? Jue
struck the seat cloth once and said: Fabricated elders, as numerous as hemp and millet. Then he brushed his sleeves and left.
Langya asked the attendant: Who is this person? He said: Senior monk Ju. Langya then personally went down, and at dawn
Passing through the hall, he asked: Are you not Venerable Ju? Do not blame the earlier offense. Ju immediately shouted, and again
asked: When did the elder arrive at Fenyang? He said: I arrived at such-and-such a time. Ju said: When I was in Zhejiang I had long heard
your name; it turns out your understanding is only like this—how did your name spread throughout the world? Langya then made a bow
and said: Huijue is at fault! The master commented: One person is like a dragon sporting in the vast ocean, making the waters of the four seas rise; one
person is like a tiger crouching at the foot of a cliff, stirring the winds of all sounds. Completely host, completely guest; completely giving life, completely taking life—the ancients
could do this; but now when host and guest meet, how is it? Bah! If you know it is like this, then stop.

Ascending the hall: Speaking freely, every phrase returns to the source; acting freely, every function accords. But as for Linji
retracting his tongue for an eon, and Deshan raising up his staff—where do they meet? After a long pause, he said: Leave your seats,
and go around the hall to drink tea.

Ascending the hall, he raised the whisk and said: If you see it here, a speck of dust flies and darkens the sky; a mustard seed
falls and covers the earth. If not, the whisk pierces the lantern, enters the pillar, and within a gnat’s eye
opens up a world. He threw down the whisk.

Ascending the hall, he cited: When Gaoting first visited Deshan, he greeted him across the river; Deshan beckoned with his hand, and Gao
Ting went straight across without looking back. Later, as Deshan’s Dharma heir, Dahui commented: Gaoting
going straight across shows he is a sharp monk; but to be Deshan’s heir—still not yet.
Why? He is still separated from Deshan by a river. Venerable Chijue said: Deshan had the poison of spitting sand to shoot people;
Gaoting, once dead, could not live again. The master commented: Deshan had the hand that wields the axe,
and Gaoting had the timber to receive it; at that time, if they had also gotten Lizhou fish broth well cooked, in one meal
the family style would not have fallen into desolation. As for Venerable Dahui and Elder Chijue speaking thus, is it
praise or blame? A strong wind reveals sturdy grass; in troubled times one knows loyal ministers.

Beginning the summer retreat, ascending the hall: The long period of one hundred twenty days—today is the first day. All of you, proceed calmly
or quickly apply effort; suddenly you may realize that the eyes are below the brows and the nose above the lips. You are only allowed
to know, not allowed to see. Want to see? He touched his nose with his hand and said: How is it?

Thanking the whisk-bearer, at the summer meal, ascending the hall: One phrase contains a hundred flavors—the temple steward has brought it forth;
one phrase is bland and tasteless—the head monk has brought it forth. It makes this whole assembly’s hunger
vanish at once; hearing and seeing both are extinguished. This mountain monk also has a flavor, only it is pungent and spicy, and I want you all to swallow
and spit it out. He struck his staff and said: How does today’s offering compare to yesterday’s?

Ascending the hall, a monk asked: Master Ma was beyond the four phrases and cut off a hundred negations; please, master, directly point out the meaning of the coming from the West.
The patriarch said: Today I am weary and cannot explain it to you. Ask Zhizang. Zhizang said:
Today I have a headache and cannot explain it to you. Ask Brother Hai. Brother Hai said: When I reach
this point, I do not understand. The monk reported this to Mazu. The patriarch said: Zang’s head is white, Hai’s head is black. The master commented:
It may be said to arise in poetry, stand in ritual, and be completed in music; but do you know the place of directly pointing for people?
After a long pause, he said: The people may be made to follow it, but not made to understand it.

Ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked Dongshan: When cold and heat come, how can one avoid them? Dongshan said: Why not go to
a place without cold or heat to avoid them? The monk said: What is the place without cold or heat? Dongshan said: When it is cold, be cold
enough to kill the monk; when it is hot, be hot enough to kill the monk. The verse says: A tune of Xinfeng, Snow-Child’s chant; clouds envelop
the ancient hall, the night deep and still; throughout heaven and earth there is no interchange—do not take the shang tone for the yu tone.

Protecting the sprouts, ascending the hall. He cited: Venerable Nanyue Rang said to Master Ma: Your study of the Dharma of mind-ground
is like planting seeds; my teaching of the essentials is like heavenly moisture; when your conditions come together, you will see
the Way. The master said: Such talk is just everyday activity—driving Guishan’s ox to plow the clouds
and part the moon; carrying Magu’s hoe, shallow planting and deep plowing, removing the weeds of ignorance and nourishing the spiritual sprouts—this is called
your study of the Dharma of mind-ground like planting seeds—understand? Thickly spreading clouds of compassion, showering sweet dew everywhere,
loftily raising the sun of wisdom, uniquely stirring the mysterious wind—this is called my teaching like heavenly moisture—understand?
When the five grains are abundant and myriad treasures flourish, the whole household is filled and all hunger is forgotten—this is called your conditions
coming together so you see the Way—understand? He slapped the meditation bench and said: It makes the Venerable of Jinzhou laugh over.

Thanking Su Xinen and Attendant Cheng Xuansheng, ascending the hall: The essential meaning lies in Kasyapa’s smile; beyond the diagrams of Fuxi
it is transmitted outside. Dragons obtain clouds and rain; hawks soar in the autumn sky.

Returning from outside the city, thanking old acquaintances, ascending the hall: At the crossroads, turning the head and mind, it is only to wait for
someone to come. At the village path, grasping the tip of the nose—the wild duck has never flown away. Seeing thus,
though caps are tipped, it is as before; if not, then west Qin and east Lu.

At the close of the summer retreat, ascending the seat. Before the fifteenth day, abide—abiding with nothing to abide in. After the fifteenth day, go,
going with nowhere to go. Right on the fifteenth day, neither going nor abiding. In the hall of original awakening, the release
of the summer meal; in Nalanda monastery, the alms bowl dance.

On the memorial day of Chan Master Fohai, ascending the hall: A single leaf drifts from a courtyard branch; the whole earth is autumn in color. Worthy of lament is
those who investigate the mystery—how many know the season? He slapped his knee and said: When Zhaozhou met Nanquan that day, he knew to say
Zhenzhou produces radishes.

Ascending the hall, he cited: A monk asked Yunmen, When the trees wither and leaves fall, what then? Yunmen said: The body reveals the golden
wind. The master commented: Responding to the time like wind, responding to the occasion like lightning is not absent, yet old Yunmen, on close inspection,
has gone too far, injuring sinews and bones.

Thanking the head monk Daji, ascending the hall: Manifest the great function, display the great activity. Breaking the target, breaking the dust, a hundred shots a hundred hits.
Establishing the true succession for ten thousand years, extinguishing the orthodox line of Dongshan. Proving that the sheep he stole
was originally in the corridor beehive.

On the first day of the ninth month, ascending the hall, he cited Ciming thanking old acquaintances in a verse: Rustling cool scenery, together with others
visiting the quiet. Boiling tea with water from below the mountain, firing the cauldron with wood from the cave. As for Ciming, he was rich yet not arrogant,
restrained yet not stingy. Even so, he was not free from expense. But the abiding original awakening here is otherwise: rustling
cool scenery, together with others visiting the quiet. Arsenic mixed with wolfsbane, white rice sprinkled with fine pepper. Tell me,
is this the same as the ancients or different?

On the Double Ninth, ascending the hall: The golden wind rustles, jade dew glistens. Purple gives more purple, yellow chrysanthemums give yellow.
Nobles and young lords, everywhere, climb heights to chant and enjoy. Those of the Way beneath the forest, in twos and threes,
gather their heads to deliberate. Tell me, what matter are they discussing? The ninth day of the ninth month is the Double Ninth.

Ascending the hall, he cited: Sanjiao instructed the assembly, saying: If you speak of this matter, the moment you raise your eyebrows you have already missed it.
Magu stepped forward and said: Missing it I will not ask; what is this matter? Sanjiao said: Missed.
Magu overturned the meditation seat, and Sanjiao immediately struck him. Xuedou commented: These two have heads but no tails;
fellows—before the eyebrows have even been raised, how can one speak of missing? A monk then asked: Why is it that the eyebrows have not
been raised? Xuedou immediately struck him. The master commented: These two great elders—one hangs before the spark of flint,
One places the hand, another leans shoulder to shoulder within the flash of lightning and its shadow—fully letting go, fully gathering in, fully giving life, fully taking life. Xuedou
Why did he say these two fellows are men with heads but no tails? Those with clear eyes must discern it.

Ascending the hall: Sakyamuni’s skull bone, Bodhidharma’s eye, in the valley it is reed valley, in the pit it is reed pit. He slapped the meditation
seat and said: It has been smashed—heaven is level, earth is level.

On the winter solstice, ascending the hall: The golden crow flies, the jade rabbit hastens, a single yang is born—this is today. He picked up his staff and said:
This rough, spicy pear, black all over like lacquer—does it still have growth or not? Then he raised his
staff and said: Seven feet, eight feet.

Ascending the hall: Lightning flashes, thunder crashes; cliffs collapse and rocks split. Not quick enough to cover the ears, not quick enough to blink—through all ages
so utterly rare—who is there who knows? Huangbo, hearing it, was startled into sticking out his tongue.

On the Buddha’s enlightenment day, ascending the hall: The old monk who cannot be frozen to death or starved to death before the mountain of perfect awakening is pressed
until the bright illumination appears—he cannot avoid deceiving and commenting on himself. Kwai! The seed of calamity remains, in both human and heavenly realms.

Circling the streets praying for clear weather, ascending the hall, and also thanking the Jingshan monastery saying: those in old stillness, those in disturbance,
if not then all is not, if so then all is so. Panshan toppled backward at the butcher’s block, Louzi in the midst of singing
suddenly awakened within the sound. You people have been circling the streets and practicing the Way these past days—could it be that there is someone on the same path and track
here? Ah! At Dragon Gate there are no overnight guests; in the tiger’s den, a tiger is born.

Ascending the hall, he cited: Manjushri is the teacher of the Seven Buddhas—why could he not rouse her from the woman’s meditative absorption? Wangming
what supernatural power did he have to bring her out of that absorption? The master said: Whether he could or could not be brought out, set that aside for now; but do you
know where the World-Honored One’s failure lies? The jade seal does not leave the emperor’s hand; how could the golden casket be for commoners?

cb:div.cb:mulu: Record of sayings of Kaiyuan Chan Monastery in Jianning Prefecture
cb:div.head: Record of sayings of Kaiyuan Chan Monastery in Jianning Prefecture
cb:div.byline: Compiled by the student of practice, Weineng Zong
cb:div.p:
Entering the abbot’s room, he picked up his staff, struck it, and said: Holding it up like this, all people under heaven submit their doubts at the sight of the wind. Leaning on
Leaning on his staff, he said: When it arrives, there is no need to point it out.

Ascending the seat, he picked up incense and completed the prayer for the sage, then said: The Buddhas do not appear in the world—clear and distinctly問答不錄
the bright moon fills the vast sky; the patriarchs did not come from the West—lonely and remote, clouds arise in the deep valley. It is as if purple ganoderma
peaks bear numinous shoots of exceptional beauty, and the painted pavilion overflows with dazzling radiance—not only within the seven prefectures is there none who can wield the staff
free from worry, but it brings all people under heaven to peaceful ease. Even so, ultimately where does the merit return?
To what place? After a long pause: a thousand peaks face Mount Hua in reverence; ten thousand streams respectfully enter the blue ocean.

He further cited Chan Master Mengbi of Jianzhou. A monk asked: What is Buddha? The answer was: I do not annotate you. The monk
said: Is that not it? The reply: You are deceiving yourself. The master’s verse says: One phrase fully presented does not deceive you;
but when you try to open your robe, you are already self-deceived. Looking more at moonlight mingled with white clouds, you do not notice the sound of springs carrying dew and
cold.

Mid-Autumn, ascending the hall. A monk stepped forward and asked: Today is the fifteenth of the eighth month—the moon is full at the doorway,
though it fills heaven and earth, not a single thread is revealed. The master said: You are sitting under an inverted basin,
so how can you find anything strange in that? The monk said: The pillar emits light and the lanterns all begin to dance. The master said: Do not yet
wear flowers. The monk said: Facing conditions, upon whom can one rely to speak of this mind? It makes one long remember Hanshan. The master said:
What did Hanshan say? The monk said: I recall that in the past an院主 asked Master Ma:
How is the Venerable’s condition these days? The master said: Sun-faced Buddha, Moon-faced Buddha. The master said: The elder has spoken it out.
The monk said: Sun-face and moon-face are hard to distinguish when they suddenly appear; clear-eyed masters see at once. The master said:
A jutting jaw at the back of the head—do not associate with it. The monk said: After seeing it, what then? The master said: Sun-faced
Buddha, Moon-faced Buddha. The monk said: A west wind blows, two or three leaves fall. The master said: In that case,
you must see Master Ma across three lives and sixty eons. The monk said: The mind-moon is solitary and round, its light swallows ten thousand forms,
the light does not illuminate objects, objects do not exist; both light and object are forgotten—what then is it? The master said: It cannot be drawn
or painted. The monk said: Is it not the moon reflected in the vast sky, rivers and streams flowing with its image? The master said: That is precisely
walking within light and shadow. The monk said: The words of Vulture Peak and the pointing at Caoxi—when have you ever recognized your own original face?
The master said: What of the moon? The monk said: As soon as you try to conceive it, black clouds obscure it; if you grasp at it, it is still not it.
The master said: Even if it burns through the skull, you still do not awaken. The monk said: If one neither grasps nor thinks, then what is it?
What about it? The master said: Make a bow.

The master cited Hanshan: My mind is like the autumn moon, its light pure and bright in a green deep pool; nothing can compare to it,
how could I possibly explain it? He commented: Since it cannot be spoken, I will cast it in a model: my mind
is like the autumn moon reflected in mid-heaven; wherever it meets, it is everywhere round. All of you, please return and sit beneath the forest,
and carefully observe before any light or shadow has arisen.

Thanking the head monk and Venerable Konggu, ascending the hall: Holding the alms bowl and occupying Nanquan’s seat—who is host? Who
is guest? Venerable, how many years have you practiced the Way? Wisdom surpasses birds and beasts; attaining the realm before the age of Vairocana,
you are still descendants of Wang the teacher—like a mouse entering and exiting its hole, what common language is there? If you wish
to illuminate the gate of great wisdom, you must first be deaf for three days. He gave a shout and said: then take
the old sword that once cut down Loulan and exchange it for a yellow ox to teach your descendants.

On the eighth day of the twelfth month, ascending the hall: Four nines are thirty-six; sleeping at night is like resting in the dew. Gautama was secretly
deceived by the stars, yet still claimed that all sentient beings are fully endowed with wisdom and virtue. Hah! This is speaking dreams with open eyes.

Thanking Venerable Nanshan of Jianguang and Venerable Beishan of Guangxiao, ascending the hall, he cited: Changqing said: Meeting a fellow practitioner on the road,
shoulder to shoulder—one’s lifelong study is thereby completed. The master commented: Zhishan and Nanshan were companions on the Way for thirty years,
in eastern and western Zhejiang, in northern and southern mountains—how many times did they gather? How many times did they meet shoulder to shoulder? Yet they never once spoke
the words “study of the Way.” Why? Because each knew shame before the other.

Ascending the hall, he cited: When Venerable Changsha returned from wandering the mountains, the head monk asked: Where have you been? Changsha said:
I have been wandering the mountains. The head monk said: Where did you go? Changsha said: At first I followed fragrant grasses away, then I followed falling
flowers back. The head monk said: It is much like the mood of spring. Changsha said: It is also better than autumn dew dripping on lotus blossoms. The verse
says: With every step taken, another step follows; casually the body is in the Wuling stream. In the cave there is endless spring
news—thus people of the time lose their way.

Thanking Head Monk Lingfeng Shiping for not accepting the invitation to Longguang, ascending the hall, he cited: Sansheng asked Xuefeng:
A golden-scaled carp that has passed through the net—what does it feed on? He commented: The Dragon Gate is ten thousand fathoms high; to pass through it completely must be云云
such a person. Elephant Bone Peak has a thousand layers; to reach the summit, one must still be the original doer. Let the ancients be for now—today’s new dragon
when Longguang and Zhifeng meet, what should they discuss? After a long pause, he said: On the road ahead, one’s body is light as a leaf,
while a great name through the ages is heavy as a mountain.

Mid-summer, ascending the hall: Half the year has passed, half the summer has passed. At the Danqing Pavilion, walking east and west, along
the continuous benches eating porridge and rice, all say everything is an already-complete public case, nothing is left undone. I ask you:
Why did Bodhidharma come to Liang, and Moteng enter the Han? Investigate!

Ascending the hall: Speaking of mind and speaking of nature, citing past and present—this all falls into entangling vines. To receive the intent before the pivot,
or to clarify the source beyond intention—this too falls into the vine thicket. Ultimately, how can it be avoided? He threw down his staff and said:
Avoid it at all costs.

Ascending the hall: The higher function, the cliff-edge phrase—beneath the three rafters one silently brings it up; at the crossroads
it stands fully revealed. He gave a shout: A swift hawk does not strike dead rabbits.

Ascending the hall, he cited: Huangbo asked Venerable Baizhang: How did the ancestral masters of old point out the teaching to people?
Baizhang sat firmly. Huangbo said: How will later generations transmit it? Baizhang said: I
thought you were a real fellow. Then he returned to his abbot’s quarters. The master commented: I have heard that Baizhang’s household rules were strict
and his gate and courtyard were lofty and steep—how was it that Huangbo’s light push made the inner hall suddenly open completely?
Even so, who in ancient or present times has truly arrived?

At the end of the summer retreat, ascending the hall: Restricting movement and dwelling in peace is like a cow-headed being eating grass; setting a deadline to attain realization is like striking the grass to startle the snake.
If one has sinews in the eyes and blood beneath the skin, then before the wind blows the nest is aware, before
the rain falls the burrow already knows it. All directions, rushing forward in great crowds—what bowl are you seeking?

Ascending the hall: The wind sighs, the rain sighs; each function matches, each thing fully transcends. If you understand it thus,
it is the twenty-fifth day of the eighth month. If not, a bag.
Opening the furnace, ascending the hall: Today the furnace is opened; there is one saying: the fire tongs and incense are all made of iron.

Ascending the hall: When silent it speaks, when speaking it is silent; iron walls layer upon layer, silver mountains stack upon stack. Xuefeng went nine times,
yet there was no gate of entry; Linji struck three times but could not penetrate it. Why? Because it is utterly clear.

Bathing the Buddha, ascending the hall: From the moment a nursing infant is born it already departs from the true principle—half sincere, half empty. Today
we do not carry out the order of Shaoshi; everyone should bail water and splash stubborn bronze.

Bidding farewell to the assembly, ascending the hall: The mountains of southern Min and the waters of western Zhejiang—so high there is no limit, so deep there is no bottom. Entering, each and every one
returns to the source; going out, nothing is not it. A single bright moon is carried on the tip of the staff, across the four seas and five lakes in the realm of the emperor
transformation.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Record of sayings of Yunyans Chan Monastery at Tiger Hill Mountain in Pingjiang Prefecture
cb:div.head: Record of sayings of Yunyans Chan Monastery at Tiger Hill Mountain in Pingjiang Prefecture
cb:div.byline: Compiled by attendants Zulu and Daojie
cb:div.p:
Entering the monastery, pointing at the Three Gates, he said: Leaving the city, you see the pagoda; entering the monastery, you climb the mountain—this is precisely a phrase outside the gate. If
what is the phrase inside the gate? Step on it and do not become angry.

Entering the abbot’s room: This place is my venerable father’s great furnace and forge, where Buddhas are cooked and ancestors refined, saints tempered and ordinary beings smelted.
This mountain monk today overturns it completely, only hoping that you will catch sight of it at a glance as soon as the wind rises. He struck the seat once.
Picking up the notice: A direct stroke under the sun, a hair-splitting blade drawn from its sheath. Orders of Wu and Yue are carried out; the wind clears the six directions.
Preface for the monastery gate: Every phrase is impartial, every point in accord. Peach blossoms in the cave; spring scenery above the roof.

Ascending the seat. Picking up incense to offer prayers for the
sage completed, he next picked up incense and said: Root and dust are utterly transcended, no branches or leaves remain, cast away beyond the wall at Bianba, where no
one in autumn can gather it—yet it is struck by a broken earthen basin, clothed in white cloud robes, clamoring and noisy, arguing public right and
public wrong; this Venerable smashes it once—today it is burned in the incense burner, also to humble him
just so. Then he took his seat. After finishing the questions and answers, he again cited: When Baoshou opened the hall, Sansheng pushed out
a monk; Baoshou immediately struck him. Sansheng said: Such conduct not only blinds this monk’s eyes,
but also blinds the eyes of all the people in the city of Zhenzhou. Baoshou threw down his staff and returned to his abbot’s quarters. The master
said: In the spark of flint light, ranks are formed to trap heroes; in the flash of lightning, drums are seized and banners
stolen—merits as high as war horses’ sweat. But as for throwing down the staff and returning to the abbot’s room, what of that? The wheel-turning king’s
three-inch iron—throughout the realm it is all blades and spears.

Minor Dharma talk: thus he said: The essential intent of Yangqi, the secret signal of Dongshan—what can be known cannot be known, wisdom cannot be問答不錄
can know it. Two thousand miles of Min mountains and Zhejiang waters, thirty days of crossing dangers and riding the waves—every place is complete truth,
and every thing is fully manifest. When one arrives before the Rain Flower Pavilion, even stubborn stones are patched; in the sound of the windlass, a sleeping tiger
lifts its eyes. My ancestors used every possible stratagem to set traps and conceal mechanisms, yet they never even accomplished half of it.
Now that this mountain monk has entered its lair, I seize its head, gather in its tail, and stroke its beard backwards. He struck the meditation
seat and said: Hūm! Hūm!

He further cited: Venerable Fifth Patriarch said: This old monk wandered for dozens of years, visiting many good spiritual friends,
thinking everything would be perfectly settled. But when I arrived at Master Yuanjian’s assembly, I simply could not open my mouth. In the end
when I reached Venerable Baiyun’s place, I bit into a single iron dumpling filling, and it instantly became endowed with a hundred flavors.
Now tell me, how would you express the one phrase of that filling? The cockscomb flower blooms, adorning the early autumn—who can
dye its purple silk threads? At times the wind stirs and they lean upon each other, as if endlessly heard before the steps. The master said:
Swallowing that one phrase of the filling whole is already nauseating—how much more so if one chews it finely and spits it out for others?
Even so, the monastics of Sichuan cannot lightly dismiss it.

Buddha’s nirvana, ascending the hall: Do you all wish to see the body of purple-gold radiance? He pointed his hand into the empty space
and said: Blue heaven! Blue heaven! Then he rubbed it and said: Wronged and suffering! Wronged and suffering! It fills people with resentment year after year,
on the fifteenth day of the second month each year.

On the first day of the third month, ascending the hall, he cited: Venerable Sanjiao said in the hall: In general, speech must accord with
At the appropriate seasonal moment, a monk stepped forth and said: "When there are four yellows and four reds, how is it?" Sanjiao said: "A March staff."
Head peach. A monk said: "Why is the belly full of stored qi?" Sanjiao said: "What about a single rope?"
A monk said: "How does one release the breath?" Sanjiao said: "It must wait until the skin is pierced." Picking it up, he said: "Timely as..."
Wind, responding to circumstances like lightning, is not without Sanjiao. This monk is like a mouse stuck to glue, caught by him.
Stuck, with nothing that can be done. At that time the mountain monk, seeing him, said: "When there are four yellows and four reds, how is it?" only
He responded to him, saying: "Dampening the robes, the apricot blossom rain is about to turn warm." "Why is the belly full of stored qi?" "Blowing on the face does not..."
Cold willow wind. How does one release the breath? As the sound harmonized, he struck.

Ascending the hall, he said: "The sea surges, the peaks are high; the sword pond waters are green. One must personally arrive there, each time and each moment, not deceive it even slightly."
Not obtainable. But consider the bustling of the three springs, with carriages and caps crowding together—was that not being personally present? Yet when it comes to being asked...
Once asked, he immediately said: "Once a year I visit the mountain temples; if not Yunyan, then Tiger Hill." Alas! The sword has gone...
It has long been gone, and only now are you marking the boat.

Ascending the hall for the Buddha-bathing ritual. Pointing at heaven and pointing at earth—what is there to rely on? I alone am honored—washing basin, washing basin.
Shaoyang Right View, the great sword Ta'a held upside down; Yaoqiao complete function, fox fur turned inside out. At Tiger Hill today...
It only requires all of you to wash him where water does not wash water. Using a whisk, he enacted the Buddha-bathing...
gesture, and said: "To attack heterodox teachings—this is harmful indeed."

Ascending the hall for the Kai Jian Qianhui Festival. Two thousand years ago, at the Vulture Peak assembly, the golden-mouth proclamation of the Humane King Protective State Sutra, one fascicle,
Today, at this Kai Jian Qianhui Festival, it is being translated again. He struck his staff.
He raised his staff and said: "Descend from the seat. The Great Buddha Hall is being established."

Ascending the hall for the summer retreat: at the beginning of the ninety-day period of restricted travel, the start of the three-month dwelling. Yunyan firmly upholds
the household rules—who would dare violate the regulations and overstep the ordinances? One is one, two is two; affairs are unified with principle, and principle is
sealed in accord with the mind. After a long silence: "The blind donkey does not receive the prediction of Lingshan."

cb:juan.cb:jhead: Recorded sayings of Venerable Yungu, volume one
Recorded sayings of Venerable Yungu, volume two

cb:div.head: Minor assembly
cb:div.p:
On the Winter Solstice: before any signs are divided, heaven is high and earth is deep. One word exhausts it all—night is dark and day is bright. Therefore
Dongshan removed and tossed back the fruit, and Ciming posted a notice in front of the monks’ hall. Exhausting one’s own mind, one laughs
at breaking others’ words. As for the rest—displaying fresh offerings, speaking of yellow and black, and sorting them out—when examined together, they all
have grown moldy and stale. Shengshou only relies on what is seen, laying it out whole on the platter—each one alike. If you
all gnaw east and gnaw west, suddenly if you bite one open and see the kernel inside, then you will know
that the one yang arises in the zi.

Cited: Yunmen instructed the assembly, saying: Before the fifteenth day I do not ask; after the fifteenth day,
bring me a phrase. The assembly was speechless. Yunmen answered on their behalf: Every day is a good day. The master commented: Yunmen
the elder speaks upside down and inside out under the clear sky in broad daylight. Shengshou also has a question: before the fifteenth day
I do not ask; after the fifteenth day, bring me a phrase. Then I would say to him: this very morning
it is the sixteenth.

New Year’s Eve: what falls away is not another thing; what moves freely is not dust. Mountains, rivers, and the great earth all manifest the Dharma
King’s body. Just as the seventy-two peaks of Dongting are high with nothing above them, beyond reach of upward gaze; Lake Tai
with more than thirty thousand qing is vast with no bottom, too deep to fathom. Can this be called the Dharma King’s body? Or not?
It is not a Dharma before the eyes, nor a matter before the eyes; from ancient times to now not even a thread can be added, nor
at year’s end can a thread be taken away. Suddenly he picked up the staff and struck it, saying: The great earth and mountains and rivers
are shattered into a hundred pieces here. Even Chiyi Zi and Tiansui Zi would dance with hands and feet,
wildly and eccentrically coming forth to say: Marvelous! Marvelous! I have long had ears but have never heard such
speech; I have had eyes but never seen such marvel. Leaning on the staff, he said: Stop! Stop! At the gate of Caoxi
no worldly talk is permitted.

Cited: Huangbo instructed the assembly, saying: All of you are men who gorge on wine dregs; wandering in this way,
how could there be today? Do you know that in the Great Tang there are no Chan masters? At that time a monk came forth from the assembly
and said: But what about those in other regions who merely lead assemblies? Huangbo said: It is not that there is no Chan,
only that there are no teachers. The master commented: Huangbo’s old woman’s heart is earnest, exhausting himself for others—truly so! It is just that
Fujian dialect does not change with past and present; many misunderstand it. Today I give a turning phrase—not only
distinguishing its dialect, but to make you see the eye of the Buddha Dharma. After a pause, he said: gelao, gelao.

At the summer retreat, informal instruction: the old regulations of Vulture Peak, the new rules of Jambudvipa—placing birds in cages, placing beasts in pens,
one must sit on top of sound and form, lie on top of sound and form; speaking freely, each step treads upon it,
what birth can be protected? what practice can be cultivated? Have you not seen Fengyang, amid war and chaos, bringing six people
to maturity of great vessels, and Miaoxi, in dangerous winds and waves, sending off thirteen people? Here at Shengshou
each one has head touching heaven and feet pressing earth; at dawn eating porridge, at mealtime eating rice. Ho! How about after practice, retreating
to drink tea?

Cited: A monk asked Master Ma: What is the meaning of the patriarch coming from the West? Master Ma said: Come closer,
and I will tell you. The monk approached; Master Ma struck him and said: Six ears cannot conspire. Later
Chan Master Huanglong Nan said: The ancients said even six ears cannot conspire, yet now in all regions three
hundreds and five hundreds, vast and surging—disaster! disaster! The master commented: The colt tramples and kills people throughout the world
that may indeed be so. But as for Huanglong speaking in this way, what is his intent? After a pause, he said: Having once been at Ba
at the place where apes cry in the gorges, before three calls are finished, the heart is already broken.

At the end of the summer retreat, informal instruction: Lake Tai soaks the moon, and the old Śākyamuni has lost his eyes and does not know; the long bridge
rides the waves, and Bodhidharma’s tongue has rotted and does not recognize it. Since there is neither knowing nor recognition, what people
resent is precisely this—unchanged from beginningless time to now. Those in all regions fix a time to attain realization and firmly cling to
wax figures—this is just binding a blind donkey and ruining future ages. This mountain monk for one summer kept and defended his boundary
and simply passed thus; all of you, in ease and accord throughout the summer, also passed thus. Even so
what, in the end, is accomplished? After a long pause, he said: The first three-threes, the later three-threes.

Cited: A monk asked the master Fengxue: After nine summers of reward, please offer your instruction. Fengxue said: A handful of fragrant
grass is not yet taken up, the six-ringed golden staff rings and shakes the sky. The master commented: Buying iron and getting gold—refined elegance
for a thousand ages. Why? Under heavy reward.

Winter Solstice, informal instruction: before the empty kalpa and after the empty kalpa, there is a phrase fully and clearly manifest.
From of old until now, no one has ever stumbled into speaking it or directly hit it with the true eye. Benjue tonight
completely breaks open the place where yin reaches its extreme and yang is born. Striking the meditation seat with the whisk, he said: the numinous light has already
shown its signs; the ten thousand things contain emptiness. One vital energy secretly pervades; flowers open and worlds arise. If you understand it thus, mid
winter is bitterly cold; if you do not understand it thus, late winter is extremely cold.

Cited: A monk asked Xiangyan: Directly cutting off the root source is what the Buddha seals. Xiangyan then threw down his staff, empty-
handed and departed. The master commented: Xiangyan directly revealed it in the open, not failing the one who came. If one strictly follows the command
and proceeds, not to mention this monk—even Śākyamuni would have no place to evade.

New Year’s Eve, informal instruction: Buddhas do not appear in the world, yet pines are naturally straight or crooked; patriarchs do not come from the West,
yet crows are necessarily black and swans necessarily white. Without cause, six years at Snowy Peak, nine years at Mount Song—on the head, blue
and around the mouth, white mold forms; making practice in coldness, its fault is still greed. As for lonely villages and leaking inns, carrying water
dragging through mud, crowded markets and masses of people, blocking streets and cutting across alleys—making practice in greed, what then of its faults? Suddenly there is
a fellow who comes out and says: Venerable, Venerable, why do you only keep measuring the ancients—what end is there to it? Just
On the thirtieth day of the twelfth month, what is the great matter each person has under their robes?
Just tell him: The east wind gradually melts the icicles under the eaves; the year’s harsh cold is now at rest.

Cited: A monk asked Zhaozhou: All dharmas return to one; where does the one return? Zhaozhou said: I was in Qingzhou making
a hemp robe weighing seven pounds. The master commented: Old man Zhaozhou only knows tears flowing from a pained gut,
not realizing his tongue is outside his mouth. If someone suddenly asked Shoushan: all dharmas return to one—where does the one return?
he would strike in response.

At the summer retreat, informal instruction: not mind, not Buddha, not things—at the decisive moment a Liao-sky falcon bursts forth;
not the wind moving, not the banner moving, not the mind moving—like a heaven-leaning long sword, chilling to the bone. If before anything is raised
one already knows; before words are spoken one already understands; even before the staff arrives one is already broken—how much less to prepare porridge and rice to receive you? Under the bright window
arranging things below, like forcing an ox to eat grass—what living path is there? This summer at Benjue there are more than sixty monks,
and affairs from fifteen prefectures and military circuits; each person’s eyes chill the lakes and seas, each one’s breath presses down heaven and earth, all of it is
discontent with Buddha and unwilling to act. Why? Meaning arises in a year of abundance.

Cited: Head Seat Ding asked Linji: What is the great meaning of the Buddha Dharma? Linji left his meditation seat, seized him
and gave him a slap, and blossoms opened. Ding stood pondering; a nearby monk said: Head Seat Ding, why do you not bow?
Ding bowed and greatly awakened. The master’s verse says: Sixty mountain canes, resentment not yet dissolved; for no reason face-to-face he meets
a stubborn head. At the decisive stroke it is like a swift wind; the Buddha Dharma must be swept away to dust.

At the end of the summer retreat, informal instruction: in Zuili the clouds are calm; among the Hundred Yue and Three Wu regions, the mountains are green and waters clear, the Doumen waves are still.
Loaded and unloaded above and below, northward and southward travel; holding fast to layered silver mountains, letting songs fill the road when released.
Old boatman for thirty years clearly understood this matter alone, but with one stroke it was scattered in Zhujing; lame Yunmen for a lifetime
looked coldly upon all regions and never left Tusita while traveling. Seeing a horn, one knows the ox—hardly one in ten thousand;
waiting by a stump for a rabbit—five pairs out of ten. Now at the time of self-restraint’s release, Shoushan opens his cloth bag,
and shakes it out completely for all of you; each of you should examine your own household goods. After a pause, if you can attain full
ten parts, it is still missing half.

Cited: One day while Weishan was sitting in the abbot’s quarters, Yangshan passed before him. Weishan said: If it were Baizhang
the elder master, you would surely have to receive a painful beating. Yangshan said: What about today’s matter? Weishan said:
put the two pieces of skin together. Yangshan said: this kindness is hard to repay. Weishan said: it is not that you are incapable, but that this old monk is aged.
Yangshan said: today I have personally seen the elder master Baizhang. Weishan said: in what phrase did you see
the former master? Yangshan said: I do not say I saw him, only that there is no separation. Weishan said: a true master from beginning to end. The master commented:
one push and one squeeze, like jade resonating and gold sounding; one release and one gather—if the head is correct and the tail is correct, it is not without merit. If
great function appears before you, not even Yangshan or Weishan can avoid striking the staff.

New Year’s Eve, informal instruction: before the flower is lifted, all phenomena throughout the cosmos are already smiling; nowhere to seek the mind,
mountains, rivers, and the great earth are already leveled and sunk. Pitifully, here and there, running east and west, like searching for sauce in a flatbread,
or seeking a seam in empty space. Little do they know that from the first day of the New Year until the
thirtieth day of the twelfth month, there is no wasted effort at all—all of it is one’s own use and enjoyment. Even so, one must never
sit here fixed. Why? This year and this very morning end, and tomorrow of next year comes.

Cited: Heze met Chan Master Qingyuan Si. Si asked: Where have you come from? Heze said: from Caoxi. Si
said: what is the meaning of Caoxi? Heze shook his body and stood. Si said: you still carry bricks and rubble. Ze
said: is there not true gold here to give people? Si said: even if there were, where would you place it? The master
commented: one is extravagant and not frugal, the other frugal and not extravagant; on careful examination, there is still no distinction
between true gold and rubble. He struck the staff and said: if seen here, even rubble can emit light. Again
he struck and said: if seen here, gold is like filth. Leaning on the staff, he said: what a pity.

cb:div.cb:mulu: General Dharma Talk with Incense Announcement
cb:div.head: General Dharma Talk with Incense Announcement
cb:div.p:
In practicing Chan and studying the Way, there is no other mysterious gateway or essential path; it must be that the person themselves awaken. If one directly
realizes it once, then seeing mountains is not mountains and seeing waters is not waters; afterward, seeing mountains is
mountains and seeing waters is waters. If one does not awaken, seeing is obstructed by seeing, turned by things and
confused by conditions; this is what is called karmic consciousness rushing about with no basis to rely on. Have you not seen the ancient virtue who said: in Chan practice one must
awaken; after awakening one must meet a teacher. If one does not meet a teacher, it is all clinging to grass and wood like spirits.
It is also said: in Chan practice there is no other path; thoroughly one must awaken oneself. Whether awakened or not, there is not the slightest difference
between them. How could this be empty words? For example, the various philosophers and the hundred crafts and skills each have their own gate of awakening.
If one attains awakening, one is naturally pure and unstained, and then there is a subtle and wondrous principle. Yan Hui’s sitting in forgetfulness,
Wei Zi said: yes—could this not be pure and unstained? Ban threw down his axe, Liang laid aside his—
is this not the subtle and wondrous principle? In our lineage, such as Deshan, who bore great sutras and treatises, opened a great lecture seat, and was revered by
students. He had annotated the Diamond Sutra commentary; upon hearing that in the south there was a teaching outside the scriptures, he became indignant
and restless, and carried his annotations out of Shu, intending to destroy its nests and dens and sweep away
its whole lineage; his bearing was very lofty. When he reached Li Prefecture near Longtan Monastery, he suddenly grew hungry and saw an old woman
selling fried cakes, so he bought a snack. The old woman said: what is it that the head seat is carrying? A hook was set in
the place of no doubt. Deshan said: the Diamond commentary—one hook and it is caught. The old woman said: I have a question;
if you can answer it, you need not pay for the fried cakes; if you cannot, then go far away and pay the price. Deshan
said: just ask it, it is truly fitting. The old woman said: the Diamond Sutra says: the past mind cannot
be found, the present mind cannot be found, the future mind cannot be found. Which mind will you point to in order to
plow and seize food? Deshan was speechless, thoroughly exposed. The old woman said: you have not yet eaten your fried cakes; now the
second iron hammer comes. Deshan said: is there no Chan monastery here? The old woman said: nearby there is Long
Tan the old master, with great eyes, driving sheep into the butcher’s gate. Deshan, unable to eat his cakes, went straight to
Longtan, rushed to the abbot’s room and asked: long have I heard of Longtan; now that I have arrived, I see neither the pond nor the dragon;
its full face is revealed. Behind the scenes Longtan replied: since you have personally arrived at Longtan,
the case is concluded. Deshan was speechless, already struck by poison. He then laid down his arms and stayed. One night
he stood serving at Longtan; late at night he went out the door and saw it was dark outside. Longtan lit a paper candle and handed it to him.
As soon as Deshan took it, Longtan immediately blew it out. At this critical moment Deshan suddenly awakened. Once losing
human life, it is never regained through countless kalpas. Deshan then burned the commentaries he had carried, saying: exhausting all subtle
distinctions is like placing a single hair into vast emptiness; exhausting the pivot of the world is like a drop thrown into a great abyss. Ancient
and modern standards. Later he stayed at Deshan, destroyed the Buddhist commentaries, and strictly enforced the true command, relying only on a single staff:
whenever he saw a monk enter the gate, he struck. His compassion was as deep as the sea, yet Yantou and Xuefeng both died under his blows.
One day Xuefeng took leave of Deshan; together with Yantou they went to the Aoshan inn. Yantou kept on sleeping,
while Xuefeng only sat without sleeping. Yantou said: why not gobble up sleep like a clay figure in the seven villages?
like that. At some future time, demons and spirits may seize men and women and carry them away. Wind arises through the grass; Feng
pointed to his chest and said: I am not settled here. One confession is already enough. Yantou said: I had thought you were
such a fellow who would later go to a solitary peak and build a grass hut, still speaking such words.
Killing people requires no knife. Feng said: I truly dare not hide it from myself; my life is already in Yantou’s hands.
Yantou said: if we go by your understanding, report everything in detail. If it is so, I will certify it for you; if not,
I will cut it off for you without lifting or carrying anything further. Feng said: when I first saw Master Yanguan expound the doctrine of form and emptiness, I had
an entry point; even the word “entry” was not necessary. Yantou said: from now on, thirty years later, you must
be careful not to bring this up. To help others, one must be utterly thorough. Also, Dongshan’s verse on crossing water says: do not follow
afar in search; with me, a thin donkey follows before and behind the horse. Yantou said: if so, you cannot even save yourself. Killing
requires seeing blood. Feng said: I once sought instruction from Deshan—within the ancestral transmission, do students still have
any share or not? He clutched the stolen goods and cried injustice. Deshan immediately struck him and said: what do you mean, dripping blood? At that moment
it was like the bottom of a bucket falling out, yet he still did not fully accept it. Deshan said: in my school there are no fixed sayings, and not a single
dharma is given to anyone; the first arrow is light, the later arrow deeper. Yantou shouted: have you not heard it said, entering through the gate
is not the family treasure—like sudden thunder too fast to cover the ears. Feng said: then how can one now seek life
when one cannot get life, or seek death when one cannot die? Yantou said: from now on, if you wish to spread the great teaching,
let everything flow from your own chest, covering heaven and covering earth, and in the end be earnest. Feng leapt down
from the meditation seat and said: today is truly Ao Mountain’s enlightenment; it is also like a poor child picking up a staff. This
is the model of practice from the ancients; without Longtan’s eye for distinguishing past and present, one is not
fit to be a teacher; and without Deshan’s direct openness that casts off reverence and formality, one is not fit
to be a disciple. Likewise, Yantou’s skill in polishing and Xuefeng’s not deceiving himself—deeply piercing and painfully
striking, bringing one to the great place of no doubt and great ease—only then are true teachers and friends seen. Since you brothers have come
here to assemble for the Way, today there is the incense announcement and general teaching. “Announcing incense” was an old practice for newly arrived monks
set up by the elders for new monks: whenever one arrived at a place in practice, one must first request instruction on conditions and causes,
and only then be allowed into the abbot’s room. Later, as people became many, it was simplified so that before the summer retreat everyone would jointly request instruction once,
once. All you brothers! From now on, if you are practicing, just in your own walking, standing, sitting, and
while lying down, investigate thoroughly; investigate again and again, until you reach the place where there is nothing left to investigate. With a crash, you will see
Deshan and Xuefeng’s very lives are in your hands. Not only those two great elders, but all buddhas from the past
and patriarchs’ lives are likewise in your grasp. When you reach here, in all twelve periods of the day you naturally gain strength,
at the shore of birth and death you also gain strength; encountering situations and conditions, you are not turned by things. In the gate of superior practitioners,
you examine and distinguish all directions, and each step has a way of emerging; you do not sit in the nest of right and wrong or gain and loss.
As for the entire Tripitaka and the seventeen hundred koans, and the subtle principles of language and words,
not a single point can be clung to. Even so, where nothing can be clung to, everything is perfectly established. Why? Because the land is numinous,
every step is grass of the Snow Mountains; every monastic is a jewel of the great ocean. You have stood long; take care.

cb:div.head: taking up the whisk
cb:div.p:
Jingshan summer retreat. The intent before sound, the upward faculty—beyond the reach of flint sparks, beyond the pursuit of lightning. Two thousand
years ago, the golden-faced elder spoke of the perfectly enlightened saṅghārāma and the wisdom of equality, taking the four noble ones and the six
realms, sentient and insentient, and binding them all in a single restraint—wrong. In the two thousand years since, successive patriarchs have appeared, taking this error
and compounding it as error; in the meantime they eat by day and sleep by night, planting millet, three hundred and five hundred, speaking east
and west, exhausting their own minds and making others laugh until their mouths split open—wrong. Two thousand years later, in the hall at Jingshan
sitting above, perfect peace is brought forth; everyone’s nose is straight and eyes horizontal, each guarding his own boundary; soon one sees the five
peaks of clouds, sitting and listening to the twin waters, penetrating top and bottom—nothing is not right, yet still: wrong. As for
carp in the mountains and dust in the well—what then? Wrong, wrong! At the decisive moment, break the black dragon’s horn
horn.

Verse on the story of Baizhang’s staff as recited by Bianquan and Yabaiyun. The master’s verse says: the mechanism of giving and taking
is directly lifted face to face; supporting heaven and earth, steep and towering; one blow to the skull shatters it into a hundred fragments, scattered
in the endless spring wind.

Leaving the summer retreat. The buddhas’ secret seal is a broken dung scoop, a stripped broom. The patriarchs’ mysterious gateway is a lump of muddy clay, an iron
threshold. Thus it is held, thus it is revealed. With hawk’s eyes and falcon’s bearing, one spies where there is no gate. Advancing forward
brings immediate peril above; retreating brings utter isolation below. Within ninety days, in the four worlds,
The wind howls; who dares violate the rules and ordinances? He raises the whisk and says: look at the seal imprint. Revealing
striking the whisk, he says: smash the mysterious barrier; whether used upright or reversed, nothing is impossible. In ten thousand miles there is not a single
blade of grass, yet a scorched-tail leaps out. When a great tiger leaves the gate it is grass; a horned venomous snake lies across the road
coiled in sleep. At just such a moment, during the summer retreat reward, who is the one who attains it? Katsu!

Case: When Guanxi first visited Linji, as soon as he stepped through the gate, Linji suddenly seized him by the chest. Xi said: I understand! I understand! Linji
then pushed him away. The master Ying’an commented: Guanxi had the bearing of a king, yet was buried alive by Linji
at the crossroads of Zhenzhou. If it had been Guangxiao at that time, even if his staff had broken, he still would not have let him go.
Why? When the household is prosperous, filial sons arise; when the state is strong, it has strategists. The abbot commented: when the string is plucked, a different tune arises;
when leaves fall, one knows autumn—so Guanxi is not without merit. If Linji had had a bit more compassion,
he would not have driven him from village to village, inn to inn. Ying’an said: even if the staff were broken, he would not have let him go. Indeed,
truly so! The master commented: Linji is like a fierce tiger blocking the road, devouring whatever he meets; it is a pity that Guanxi’s life
ended beneath its claws and fangs. If at that time he had skillfully stroked its whiskers backward, not only would he have escaped being seized,
he would also have avoided later criticism. Even so, where is it that Linji lacked even a little
compassion? Tiger stripes are easy to see; human markings are hard to see.

cb:div.head: Blood-written Lotus Sutra
cb:div.p:
Beneath the eyes there are tendons and skin there is blood; only oneself knows the prick of the needle. On the tip of a hair one can open
transformation; red lotus blossoms, three or four branches.

cb:div.head: Tianyi Enlightenment Well
cb:div.p:
The cloud-root is dug through, deep and dark green; Tianyi suffers and is deceived deeply; mud and water cover the whole body with no place for snow
to settle; in the moonlight, the shadow of a wild goose falls into the heart of the waves.

cb:div.head: Name of the Southern Elder
cb:div.p:
The barbarian tongue of the Pu mouth erases the true transmission; throughout the Jambudvīpa realm, all are descendants. Even Mañjuśrī’s pointing cannot
reach it; even Śubhākarasiṃha is dragged into stumbling backward.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Sent to Venerable Shixi of Lingyin
cb:div.head: Sent to Venerable Shixi of Lingyin
cb:div.p:
The snow-lit clarity fully wields the command of life and death; I myself have been pressed to the edge of death. Now when I reflect on it,
my hairs still stand on end, helplessly.

cb:div.cb:mulu: A skull carries the burden of self and other
cb:div.head: A skull carries the burden of self and other
cb:div.p:
One thing, a hundred limbs, no difference between self and other; bearing it and being borne—when will it end? If you let it go now, then truly let it
go; one burden never had two ends to begin with.

cb:div.head: Mourning Venerable Shuangshan
cb:div.p:
When the sea surges and waves rise, the sword transforms into a dragon; no need to grieve over the empty blue pool. By the pine window in cold moonlight, I ponder
it further; even a withered tree still chants in the midnight wind.

cb:div.head: Stone Mirror Title
cb:div.p:
One move at the decisive moment rises on a sheer cliff; clearly it is neither form nor platform. Looking at it, grasping or letting go are all delusions
exhausted; ashamed to face one’s original face at birth.

cb:div.head: Title “Not Transmitted”
cb:div.p:
Smashing the winter-melon seal of India, sweeping away the “child Zen” of China—everything flows out from the chest,
from the very source, extinguishing the blind-donkey edge of the true lineage.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Jingshan: After the Fire, Reopening the Sayings of Dahui
cb:div.head: Jingshan: After the Fire, Reopening the Sayings of Dahui
cb:div.p:
The blocks together number more than eight hundred pieces, with over thirty million characters. In the flash of flint fire, one can see it clearly;
once it is broken through, the old master’s three-inch tongue still remains.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Title “Shepherd Slope” (from the Su province scholar’s idea of herding sheep)
cb:div.head: Shepherd Slope Title蘇省元取牧羊之義
cb:div.p:
Holding the Han envoy’s tally, he entered the enemy court; where the ram slept, the grass was deep. Even today the flat ground has become mounds,
all of it is the single heart of loyal devotion.

cb:div.head: Seeing off Layman Hui
cb:div.p:
The Tathāgata and the heavenly prince were originally fellow practitioners; having mouths, they have always kept them sealed. At this sudden parting, leakage occurs
you bow deeply, and I respond with palms together in greeting.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Seeing off the young monk Wuji, Brother Ming
cb:div.head: Seeing off the young monk Wuji, Brother Ming
cb:div.p:
Before even unfolding the cooking cloth, you were already pushed away, yet made to sit and drink tea. Such full courtesy and earnestness
has led you to be spoiled by your teacher.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Presented to Chen Meipo, Historian Lecturer
cb:div.head: Presented to Chen Meipo, Historian Lecturer
cb:div.p:
The realm is fully restored, rejoicing in times of peace; who could draw down the Milky Way to wash away weapons of war? It is good to speak of turning oxen loose and returning horses to pasture
this affair—so all under heaven may happily cut firewood and till the fields.

cb:div.head: Writing the Flower Garland Sutra
cb:div.p:
Two thousand years of affairs have already become blurred; white paper is taken up and written with black ink. The slight intent of Sudhana’s quest is fully revealed,
from beginning to end, success and failure are all Mañjuśrī.

cb:div.head: Bodhisattva seated on a rock
cb:div.p: On blue cliffs rising ten thousand ren, and waterfalls plunging a thousand fathoms; seated on a broad boulder, his compassion is as deep as the sea.

cb:div.head: Fish Basket
cb:div.p: Clouds hang in tangled folds, and the bearing is such as this; intent on the brocade-scaled fish, one misses the place of complete transmission.

cb:div.head: Grass-robed Mañjuśrī
cb:div.p:
Hair hanging to the shoulders, robe stitched of grass; holding the ruyi scepter—what does it illuminate? I bow to the teacher of the seven buddhas; in Vimalakīrti’s city he is met
and overthrown.

The ocean of practice and vows is deep without bottom; the sutra in hand is clearly shown. A bottle pours out two thousand repayments, falling into the grass
there.

cb:div.head: Jiashan meets Chuanzi
cb:div.p: Revered one, at which temple do you dwell? Nets are cast across the sky. At the place of three nods, a few more boat-poles are still lacking.

cb:div.cb:mulu: Xiangyan hears the strike of bamboo
cb:div.head: Xiangyan hears the strike of bamboo
cb:div.p: A single pebble is heavier than a thousand jun; at the moment of confrontation it triggers the mechanism. After the skull is shattered, lingering regret remains in the bamboo’s fading echo.

cb:div.head: Morning Sun
cb:div.p: The present mechanism runs through a single thread; what is precious is its completeness, with no leakage or flaw. Pointing to the numinous and the cold, the whole body breaks into cold sweat.

cb:div.head: Facing the Moon
cb:div.p: In the hand is a palm-leaf scripture; its circulation and transmission have never been fully penetrated. Calling to the moon rabbit in heat—characters are black, paper is white.

cb:div.head: Hanshan執掃󰆠
